text: In Japanese, how do you say 'Good morning'? {{c1::おはようございます (Ohayō gozaimasu)}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: おはようございます (Ohayō gozaimasu) | Meaning: Good morning
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good morning' en Japanese? おはようございます (Ohayō gozaimasu)

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'Hello / Good afternoon'? {{c1::こんにちは (Konnichiwa)}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: こんにちは (Konnichiwa) | Meaning: Hello / Good afternoon
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Hello / Good afternoon' en Japanese? こんにちは (Konnichiwa)

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'Good evening'? {{c1::こんばんは (Konbanwa)}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: こんばんは (Konbanwa) | Meaning: Good evening
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good evening' en Japanese? こんばんは (Konbanwa)

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'Goodbye'? {{c1::さようなら (Sayōnara)}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: さようなら (Sayōnara) | Meaning: Goodbye
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Goodbye' en Japanese? さようなら (Sayōnara)

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'Nice to meet you'? {{c1::はじめまして (Hajimemashite)}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: はじめまして (Hajimemashite) | Meaning: Nice to meet you
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Nice to meet you' en Japanese? はじめまして (Hajimemashite)

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'Good night'? {{c1::おやすみなさい (Oyasumi nasai)}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: おやすみなさい (Oyasumi nasai) | Meaning: Good night
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Good night' en Japanese? おやすみなさい (Oyasumi nasai)

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'See you / Bye - Informal'? {{c1::じゃあね (Jā ne)}}
explanation: Vocabulary for the topic 'Greetings and Farewells'. Level: A1.
usage: Term: じゃあね (Jā ne) | Meaning: See you / Bye - Informal
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'See you / Bye - Informal' en Japanese? じゃあね (Jā ne)

text: In Japanese, the general greeting 'Hello' used in the afternoon is '{{c1::こんにちは}}' (Konnichiwa).
explanation: Note that the final 'wa' sound is written with the hiragana 'ha' (は) because it historically starts a topic clause.
usage: Term: こんにちは (Konnichiwa) | Use: Day greeting.
spanish: En japonés, el saludo general 'Hola' en la tarde es 'こんにちは' (Konnichiwa).

text: In Japanese, the polite way to say 'Good morning' is '{{c1::おはようございます}}' (Ohayou gozaimasu).
explanation: Informally (with friends or family), you can simply say 'おはよう' (Ohayou).
usage: Polite: おはようございます | Informal: おはよう
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Buenos días' formalmente en japonés? おはようございます (Ohayou gozaimasu).

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'Good evening'? {{c1::こんばんは}} (Konbanwa)
explanation: Like Konnichiwa, the final 'wa' sound is written with the topic marker particle 'ha' (は).
usage: Term: こんばんは (Konbanwa)
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Buenas noches (al llegar)' en japonés? こんばんは (Konbanwa).

text: In Japanese, the polite way to say 'Good night' (when going to bed) is '{{c1::おやすみなさい}}' (Oyasuminasai).
explanation: Informally, you can say 'おやすみ' (Oyasumi).
usage: Polite: おやすみなさい | Informal: おやすみ
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Buenas noches (al despedirse o ir a dormir)' en japonés? おやすみなさい (Oyasuminasai).

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'Goodbye' formally (implying a long separation)? {{c1::さようなら}} (Sayounara)
explanation: Often avoided with coworkers or daily contacts because it implies a final or long farewell. Classrooms and friends use shorter, informal words.
usage: Term: さようなら | Informal alternatives: またね (See you), じゃあね (Bye).
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Adiós' formalmente en japonés? さようなら (Sayounara).

text: In Japanese, when meeting someone for the first time, you start with the greeting '{{c1::はじめまして}}' (Hajimemashite), which means 'Nice to meet you'.
explanation: Derived from the verb 'hajimeru' (to begin). Used strictly at the very beginning of a self-introduction.
usage: Term: はじめまして (Hajimemashite)
spanish: Al presentarse en japonés, se empieza con 'はじめまして' (Hajimemashite), que significa 'Encantado de conocerle'.

text: In Japanese, at the end of a self-introduction, you say '{{c1::どうぞよろしくおねがいします}}' (Douzo yoroshiku onegaishimasu), which translates to 'Please be kind to me'.
explanation: An essential cultural phrase showing respect and requesting a good relationship.
usage: Term: どうぞよろしくおねがいします | Casual: よろしく
spanish: Al final de una presentación en japonés, se dice 'どうぞよろしくおねがいします' (Douzo yoroshiku onegaishimasu), que equivale a 'Mucho gusto / Cuento con usted'.

text: In Japanese, the polite way to say 'Please' (when asking for an action or object) is '{{c1::おねがいします}}' (Onegaishimasu) or '{{c2::ください}}' (Kudasai).
explanation: Onegaishimasu is more formal and can be used on its own. Kudasai is appended to items or verbs in the -te form.
usage: Examples: お水をお願いします。 (Water, please.) | これ、ください。 (This one, please.)
spanish: En japonés, para decir 'Por favor', se usa 'おねがいします' (Onegaishimasu) o 'ください' (Kudasai).

text: In Japanese, the polite way to say 'Thank you very much' is '{{c1::ありがとうございます}}' (Arigatou gozaimasu).
explanation: Informally (with close peers), you can say just 'ありがとう' (Arigatou) or even 'どうも' (Doumo).
usage: Polite: ありがとうございます | Informal: ありがとう
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'Muchas gracias' en japonés? ありがとうございます (Arigatou gozaimasu).

text: In Japanese, how do you say 'You're welcome'? {{c1::どういたしまして}} (Douitashimashite)
explanation: The standard textbook response to 'Arigatou'. In natural conversation, speakers often say 'いいえ' (Iie - No problem).
usage: Term: どういたしまして
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice 'De nada' en japonés? どういたしまして (Douitashimashite).

text: In Japanese, how do you ask 'What is your name?' politely? {{c1::お名前は何ですか}} (O-namae wa nan desu ka)
explanation: The prefix 'o-' is honorific, expressing respect towards the other person.
usage: Dialogue: - お名前は何ですか。 - ジーザスです。
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Cuál es su nombre?' en japonés? お名前は何ですか (O-namae wa nan desu ka).

text: In Japanese, how do you ask 'How are you? / Are you well?'? {{c1::お元気ですか}} (O-genki desu ka)
explanation: Used when you haven't seen someone for a while. Not used daily like 'how are you' in English.
usage: Dialogue: - お元気ですか。 - はい、元気です。
spanish: ¿Cómo se dice '¿Cómo estás? / ¿Está usted bien?' en japonés? お元気ですか (O-genki desu ka).

text: In Japanese, '{{c1::すみません}}' (Sumimasen) is a highly useful word that means {{c2::Excuse me / Sorry / Thank you}}.
explanation: Used to get attention, apologize, or thank someone for going to trouble for you.
usage: Example: すみません、ビルはどこですか？ (Excuse me, where is the building?)
spanish: En japonés, 'すみません' (Sumimasen) significa 'Disculpe / Lo siento / Gracias' según el contexto.
